behaviors.description: Traveling-salesman heuristic route length stays within 1.5x of optimal for up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm itself (single computation boundary), not module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocks are unnecessary; this is pure computation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is strictly about route-length quality of the heuristic, not service integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run deterministic algorithm tests on fixed stop sets (sizes 5-20) with precomputed optimal lengths; assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: Delivery time windows are computed correctly from leg distance and configured road-type average speed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is formula/lookup logic in estimator code. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing does not hide the arithmetic or speed-selection bug when inputs are explicit. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on computation correctness from provided distance+speed inputs, which can be validated at function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call the time-estimation function with table-driven leg distances and road types mapped to configured speeds; assert per-leg and total window durations match expected values (including rounding rules).

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes exceeding remaining shift time and returns exact excess.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is local validation logic (compare totals, compute excess). REAL-INFRA: no external infrastructure involved. MOCK-HIDE: mocking does not mask this failure mode. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is purely validator output for given totals.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test validator with boundary cases (equal, just over, far over, zero remaining) and assert reject/accept plus precise excess duration.

behaviors.description: If one address fails geocoding (no results), service skips it, optimizes remaining stops, and reports skipped addresses.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (geocoder result handling -> optimizer input -> reporting), not a single function. REAL-INFRA check: no real network required; an in-process geocoder stub returning 'no results' reproduces the behavior, so not System. MOCK-HIDE: fully mocking the whole service would hide this; wiring-level integration catches it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text specifies a deterministic API 'no results' case, not real HTTP conditions/timeouts.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with real internal modules plus stub geocoding adapter that fails one address; assert failed address is listed in skipped report and final route excludes it while still optimizing the remaining addresses.

behaviors.description: Duplicate input addresses are consolidated into one stop with merged delivery items and are not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug typically emerges across preprocessing/aggregation and routing modules (state handoff). REAL-INFRA check: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only dedup helper can miss duplicate visitation after route construction; integrated module test is needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is local service orchestration without real infra or AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run service pipeline with duplicate addresses carrying different items; assert output route contains one stop for that address, items are combined, and visit sequence includes that location once.
